Meet Rene Saul Escobar Ochoa. He’s an MS-13 terrorist and Salvadoran FUGITIVE wanted for:
👉Ordering gang members to commit multiple murders
👉Extortion
👉Imprisonment
👉Drug trafficking

We found him alongside an MS-13 kingpin wanted for FIVE MURDERS and other serious crimes.

These two were living in Nebraska within the Omaha community.

“Our ICE officers and agents are protecting your neighborhoods, even when you don’t know the threat is there, so either support them or get out of the way.”

—Acting ICE Director Todd M. Lyons

📰Learn more about this case and more in our newsroom.

Keep your family safe during the holidays: all travelers entering the United States are REQUIRED to DECLARE meats, fruits, vegetables, plants, seeds, soil, animals, as well as plant and animal products they may be carrying.

On Sunday, October 12, threatening messages were discovered graffitied on the USCIS building in Williston, Vermont. Vandalizers wrote, “kill yourselves,” “your not safe were coming,” and “die,” on our federal building.

We are not going to allow domestic terrorists to attack our law enforcement. DHS has already arrested dozens of Antifa-aligned left-wing violent extremists who have attacked law enforcement, murdered innocent civilians, and launched a wave of violent riots throughout the Unites States.

Secretary Noem’s message to the rioters is clear: you will not stop us or slow us down.

Federal law enforcement and the Williston Police Department are actively investigating these threats.

items.tweet_text:
HERE’S THE REAL STORY: During the October 25 operation, Border Patrol agents were surrounded and boxed in by a group of agitators. Federal law enforcement issued multiple lawful commands and verbal warnings, all of which were ignored.

While trying to safely clear the area, a grey sedan sped down the road and boxed in Border Patrol. Border Patrol gave repeated warnings to the driver to back his car up which he refused.

After spewing profanities at law enforcement, he proceeded to charge out of his car toward law enforcement and resisted arrest. During the arrest another US citizen assaulted the arresting agent, striking him in the head. Both were arrested.

The operation resulted in the arrest of a criminal illegal alien from Mexico who has previously been arrested for assault.  Secretary Noem’s message to the rioters is clear: you will not stop us or slow us down. DHS and our federal law enforcement partners will continue to enforce the law.

items.tweet_text:
A criminal illegal alien from Mexico, has been arrested in relation to Saturday’s shooting targeting Border Patrol agents in Chicago. The suspect has a previously been convicted for aggravated unlawful use of a weapon/vehicle, felony possession of a weapon, and illegal entry.

He is currently marked as a violator of the Laken Riley Act, pending charges related to assaulting officers. 
 
This incident is not isolated and reflects a growing and dangerous trend of violence and obstruction. Over the past two months, we’ve seen an increase in assaults and obstruction targeting federal law enforcement during operations. These confrontations highlight the dangers our agents face daily and the escalating aggression toward law enforcement.

items.tweet_text:
Every American needs to see this.

18-year-old Sheridan Gorman was brutally murdered in Chicago — just blocks from her college dorm — by an illegal alien who should never have been here.

At 1:30 a.m., while watching the northern lights near Lake Michigan, a masked gunman opened fire. Sheridan warned her friends and ran — only making it about 40 feet before she was shot in the back and killed in cold blood.

Her devastated parents are calling it what it is: "It was a preventable murder."

The killer was released into the U.S. under Biden, arrested for shoplifting in sanctuary Chicago, released without notifying ICE, and then skipped court.

Her father demands accountability: "When someone commits a crime and they've been here illegally, there needs to be cooperation... He should've been handed over to the feds."

Open borders and sanctuary policies killed Sheridan Gorman.

This is the human cost of failed leadership.

items.tweet_text: .@POTUS: "Honoring the British King might seem an ironic beginning to our celebration of 250 years of American independence — but in fact, no tribute could be more appropriate. Long before Americans had a nation or Constitution, we first had a culture, a character, and a creed. Before we ever proclaimed our independence, Americans carried within us the rarest of gifts: moral courage, and it came from a small but mighty kingdom from across the sea."

items.tweet_text: The @USNationalGuard shooting was a terrorist action that was a direct result of unchecked and unvetted migration from Afghanistan. We are taking decisive action - all final decisions for asylum have been shut down, we've paused adjudication of immigrant and non immigrant benefits for any alien from the 19 countries of concern, and we will review all existing green cards that were granted in the last 5 years.

Weston was a bright young American whose future was stolen by a poison that should never have been in our country in the first place.

Under the leadership of @POTUS Trump, we continue to fight to dismantle drug cartels from pouring poison into our country and killing Americans.

At JFK Airport, as travelers waited in hours-long lines, an infant became unresponsive and stopped breathing. The panic of the child’s family and nearby passengers were heard by an ICE agent stationed at a checkpoint.

This heroic officer immediately sprang into action—rushing toward the cries, taking the child, and performing a Heimlich maneuver that restored the infant’s breathing after nearly two minutes.

This officer’s extraordinary bravery embodies the selfless service of DHS law enforcement.

items.tweet_text:
Our ICE officers and agents are HEROES.

On March 25, 2026, an ICE agent helping to support @TSA operations at John F. Kennedy International Airport in New York jumped into action to help save the life of a one-year-old child experiencing a medical emergency.

The heroic actions of this officer began when a one-year-old child became unresponsive in the arms of his father, unable to breathe for almost two minutes. CCTV review shows a passenger in a TSA Precheck line holding his one-year-old in his arms when the child’s arms go lifeless; panic ensues, and the father is seen scrambling around the area, and calling for help.  

The agent working at his post, heard the screams from the father and other passengers and sprinted to the scene. The father handed the child to the officer, who then assessed the unresponsive child and began performing the Heimlich maneuver. After a few seconds, the child started breathing again. EMS personnel arrived on scene with medical equipment to further monitor and re-assess the child. The child was re-assessed and determined to be healthy enough to fly.

items.tweet_text:
Jocelyn Nungaray should be a teenager now.

Instead, she was sexually assaulted and brutally murdered in June 2024 by two Venezuelan nationals in the country illegally.

Thanks to ICE’s VOICE Office, immigration crime victims and their families are not alone.

Our skilled liaisons:
 
—Help victims understand the immigration enforcement and removal process
—Provide victims with access to social service professionals and resources
—Help victims receive custody status updates on criminal aliens 

Call 855-48-VOICE, Monday-Friday 8 A.M. to 8 P.M. ET or visit us online at http://ICE.gov/VOICE.

Celebrating the one-year anniversary on April 9 of the Trump Administration's VOICE Office relaunch.

items.tweet_text:
Megan Bos’ future was erased by an illegal alien who had no right to be in the U.S.
 
Today marks the one-year anniversary of the day authorities found Megan’s body — covered in bleach in a Chicago suburb dumpster. Horrific.
 
ICE thanks Jennifer Bos, Megan’s mother, for the tremendous courage she showed Thursday while sharing her story of unbearable loss.
 
Thanks to the ICE VOICE office, Megan will never be forgotten, and Jennifer will never be alone.

NORTH DAKOTA METH RINGLEADER GETS 40 YEARS AFTER ICE HSI PROBE

Jessica Trottier will spend decades in prison and forfeit $38,000 for leading a criminal organization that distributed methamphetamine across several states and being a felon caught with a firearm.

Homeland Security Task Force partners @ICEgov, @ATFHQ and @DEAHQ investigated the case.

The NYC district attorney called the deadly arson attack “an act of mass murder” after the suspect watched people jump from their burning apartments while sipping a beer.

Despite this horrific incident, NYC remains adamant it will not honor the ICE detainers that keep murderers and other criminals from returning to the city’s streets.

This goes beyond politics. It’s time to protect Americans.

items.tweet_text:

items.tweet_text:
Las Vegas police arrested John Young Cotter Johnstone, an illegal alien from the UK, who police say was captured on video dragging and swinging dogs by their leashes and kicking one dog.

Johnstone faces multiple charges connected to animal abuse he allegedly committed while working at a local rescue in Nevada.

The same day Johnstone was arrested, ICE issued an immigration detainer with the Clark County Detention Center. The next day, April 2, Clark County officials honored the detainer, and ICE took custody of the alleged animal abuser.

Johnstone came to the United States under the Visa Waiver Program as a visitor. He was supposed to leave the country by Feb. 22, 2022. 

ICE secured a final order of removal. He’ll stay in ICE detention pending local charges.

items.tweet_text: NEC Director Kevin Hassett: "President Trump, in the Big Beautiful Bill, attempted to convince Congress to modernize the gas tax, and account for the fact that, for example, electric vehicles don't pay it, but they actually produce more wear and tear on the highways because they're heavier... that could allow for the gas tax to go down, if you were to equalize that... I think @POTUS was right to advocate that then, and if we had done that then, we'd be in much better shape right now."

"40,000 estimated gallons a day are coming out of this thing... it needs to be fixed."

"The color, 'American Flag Blue,' is meant to enhance the reflectivity... You want to have a dark base. There was a time in history when this Reflecting Pool was actually asphalt black."

So I think that they are on their last legs."

.@SecretaryBurgum at the Pikka development on the North Slope, says Alaska will play "a huge role going forward in the major shift in the geopolitics of energy."

"It is 8 days from Anchorage to Tokyo, and most of that is along the Aleutian Islands. That's all U.S.-controlled territorial waters... vs. right now, a country like Japan getting 92% of their oil from the Strait of Hormuz, and it can be a 30, 35-day trip on a good day—or maybe not at all."

items.tweet_text:
🚨UPDATE: Police disclosed more information about their arrest of 21-year-old Guatemalan illegal alien Henry Roldan Perez-Roblero. 

Perez-Roblero faces a felony charge in Florida for allegedly sexually assaulting a child under 12 — a capital felony punishable in the state by death.

ICE issued a detainer with law enforcement in Martin County, Florida, and will take custody of him if he is ever released.

➡️Under @POTUS @realDonaldTrump and @SecMullinDHS, if you break the law, you will face the consequences. Criminal illegal aliens are not welcome in the U.S.

items.tweet_text: This holiday season, we’re saying thanks where it matters most: to our #DHSWorkforce. From disaster response and election security to safeguarding AI, combatting fentanyl, protecting utilities, and securing our airways, these dedicated professionals work tirelessly to protect our nation. Join us all week for #5DaysofThanks as we honor their service. #WeAreDHS
